mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts yield positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising industrial production fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300, inflation expectations become entrenched, prompting market repricing of equity risk premiums and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 300 coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market slack and underemployment, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence yields positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns in greater than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side stress and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops exceeding 1.0% are followed by positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60% of instances.
